# Tasks: Piano Center Management System

**Input**: Design documents from `/specs/001-piano-center-management/`
**Prerequisites**: plan.md, spec.md, research.md, data-model.md, contracts/

**Tests**: Not explicitly requested in the spec — test tasks are NOT generated by default. A small set of contract/E2E smoke tests is included in the Polish phase as recommended hygiene; expand these via `/speckit-tasks` if a TDD-first workflow is desired.

**Organization**: Tasks are grouped by user story (10 stories from spec.md). Each story is independently implementable and testable once the foundational phase is complete.

## Format: `[ID] [P?] [Story] Description`

- **[P]**: Can run in parallel (different files, no incomplete dependencies)
- **[Story]**: Which user story the task belongs to (US1 … US10)
- All paths are relative to the repository root (`/home/lehiep/trackedux/`)

> **Note on the 2026-04-27 clarifications**: The class data model was simplified — no `class_type`, no `max_students`, classes have a `name` and `duration_minutes`. The Package entity stores `price` (VND, BIGINT). Sessions persist until manual logout. Makeup sessions get a visual badge on the calendar. Tasks below reflect this final shape; if you are adjusting an existing implementation, the dedicated migration is **T067** in Phase 4 (US2).

---

## Phase 1: Setup (Shared Infrastructure)

**Purpose**: Project initialization, tooling, and base configuration

- [ ] T001 Create the two-tree project layout (`backend/` and `frontend/`) with shared `README.md` and `.gitignore` (covering `node_modules/`, `dist/`, `__pycache__/`, `.venv/`, `.env`)
- [ ] T002 Initialize backend Python project in `backend/pyproject.toml` with dependencies: `fastapi>=0.115`, `uvicorn[standard]>=0.30`, `sqlalchemy[asyncio]>=2.0`, `asyncpg>=0.29`, `pydantic>=2.0`, `pydantic-settings>=2.0`, `python-jose[cryptography]>=3.3`, `passlib[bcrypt]>=1.7.4`, `bcrypt==4.0.1`, `alembic>=1.13`, `python-multipart>=0.0.9`; dev extras `pytest`, `pytest-asyncio`, `httpx`, `testcontainers[postgres]`, `ruff`
- [ ] T003 [P] Initialize frontend project in `frontend/package.json` with Vite + React 19; runtime deps: `react`, `react-dom`, `react-router-dom@7`, `antd@6`, `@ant-design/icons`, `react-i18next`, `i18next`, `@tanstack/react-query@5`, `axios`, `dayjs`, `@fullcalendar/react`, `@fullcalendar/core`, `@fullcalendar/daygrid`, `@fullcalendar/timegrid`, `vite-plugin-pwa`; dev deps: `vite@8`, `@vitejs/plugin-react`, `eslint`, `eslint-plugin-react-hooks`, `eslint-plugin-react-refresh`, `@playwright/test`
- [ ] T004 [P] Configure backend linting in `backend/ruff.toml` (line-length 120, target Python 3.11)
- [ ] T005 [P] Configure frontend linting in `frontend/eslint.config.js` (React 19, hooks rules, prettier-compatible)
- [X] T006 [P] Create `backend/.env.example` with `DATABASE_URL`, `JWT_SECRET`, `JWT_ALGORITHM=HS256`, `ACCESS_TOKEN_TTL_DAYS=30`, `REFRESH_TOKEN_TTL_DAYS=90`, `DEFAULT_LANGUAGE=vi` (long-lived tokens reflect the no-idle-timeout clarification)
- [ ] T007 [P] Create `frontend/.env.example` with `VITE_API_BASE_URL=http://localhost:8000/api/v1`
- [ ] T008 [P] Configure PWA in `frontend/vite.config.js` via `vite-plugin-pwa` (manifest, service worker for asset caching only, install prompt, auto-update)
- [ ] T009 [P] Configure Playwright in `frontend/playwright.config.js` (headless Chromium, base URL from env, single worker)
- [ ] T010 Create `backend/Dockerfile` and `docker-compose.yml` at repo root (Postgres 16 + backend service for local dev)

---

## Phase 2: Foundational (Blocking Prerequisites)

**Purpose**: Core infrastructure that MUST exist before any user story can be implemented

**⚠️ CRITICAL**: No user story work can begin until this phase is complete

### Backend foundation

- [ ] T011 Create FastAPI app entrypoint in `backend/app/main.py` (CORS, exception handlers, OpenAPI metadata, `/api/v1` prefix)
- [ ] T012 Create config module in `backend/app/core/config.py` using `pydantic-settings` (loads `.env`, exposes `Settings`)
- [ ] T013 Create async DB engine + session factory in `backend/app/db/session.py` and base declarative class in `backend/app/db/base.py`
- [ ] T014 Initialize Alembic in `backend/alembic.ini` and `backend/alembic/env.py` (async engine, sequential numbered revisions)
- [ ] T015 Create migration `backend/alembic/versions/001_init_extensions.py` (uuid-ossp, unaccent, pg_trgm)
- [ ] T016 Create migration `backend/alembic/versions/002_enum_types.py` for PostgreSQL enums: `user_role`, `enrollment_status`, `payment_status`, `attendance_status`, `notification_type`, `notification_channel`, `notification_status` (note: no `class_type` enum per clarification 2026-04-27)
- [ ] T017 Create JWT helpers in `backend/app/core/security.py` (`create_access_token`, `create_refresh_token`, `decode_token`, password hashing via passlib)
- [ ] T018 Create FastAPI auth dependencies in `backend/app/core/deps.py` (`get_current_user`, `require_role(...)`, RBAC helpers for admin/staff/parent)
- [ ] T019 Create User model in `backend/app/models/user.py` (id UUID, username UNIQUE, email, password_hash, role enum, full_name, language, is_active, timestamps)
- [ ] T020 Create migration `backend/alembic/versions/003_users.py` (User table with indexes on username, email, role)
- [ ] T021 Create User Pydantic schemas in `backend/app/schemas/user.py` (UserCreate, UserRead, UserLogin, TokenResponse)
- [X] T022 Create auth router in `backend/app/api/auth.py` with `POST /auth/login`, `POST /auth/refresh`, `POST /auth/logout`, `GET /auth/me` (long-lived tokens per clarification 2026-04-27 — no per-request idle TTL)
- [ ] T023 Create base API router that mounts feature routers in `backend/app/api/__init__.py`
- [ ] T024 Create error handling and structured logging in `backend/app/core/logging.py` (JSON logs in prod, human-readable in dev)
- [ ] T025 Create seed script `backend/app/scripts/seed_admin.py` to bootstrap an initial admin user from env vars

### Frontend foundation

- [ ] T026 Create Vite entry `frontend/src/main.jsx` and `frontend/index.html` with React 19 root
- [ ] T027 [P] Create i18n config in `frontend/src/i18n/index.js` (react-i18next, lazy namespaces, default `vi`, fallback `en`)
- [ ] T028 [P] Create initial translation files `frontend/src/i18n/vi.json` and `frontend/src/i18n/en.json` with `common`, `auth`, `validation` namespaces
- [ ] T029 [P] Create axios client in `frontend/src/api/client.js` (base URL from env, JWT interceptor, 401 → refresh flow → logout)
- [ ] T030 [P] Create React Query provider wrapper in `frontend/src/api/queryClient.js` (default `staleTime`, retry policy)
- [ ] T031 Create app shell in `frontend/src/App.jsx` (Ant Design ConfigProvider with vi locale, React Query provider, Router, AuthProvider, i18n provider)
- [ ] T032 Create routing config in `frontend/src/routes/index.jsx` (admin/staff vs parent layouts, protected routes, 404)
- [X] T033 Create AuthContext + token storage in `frontend/src/auth/AuthContext.jsx` and `frontend/src/auth/tokenStorage.js` (localStorage; tokens persist until logout per clarification 2026-04-27)
- [ ] T034 Create login page in `frontend/src/auth/LoginPage.jsx` (Ant Design Form, username/password, language switcher in header)
- [ ] T035 Create admin/staff layout in `frontend/src/components/layouts/AdminLayout.jsx` (Header with logo + nav + LanguageSwitcher + user menu, Sider with navigation, Content area, mobile bottom-nav)
- [ ] T036 Create LanguageSwitcher component in `frontend/src/components/LanguageSwitcher.jsx` (toggle vi/en, persist to localStorage and to user profile)
- [ ] T037 Create route guard component in `frontend/src/auth/RequireAuth.jsx` (redirects to `/login` if no valid token; checks role for role-restricted routes)

**Checkpoint**: Foundational infrastructure complete — backend runs, DB migrated, admin can log in and reach an empty admin shell. User-story phases can now begin (P1 stories may run in parallel across developers).

## Phase 3: User Story 1 — Admin Manages Students (Priority: P1) 🎯 MVP

**Goal**: Admin/staff can register students with full profile fields, sort/filter, and update enrollment status. Staff cannot see parent contact details or tuition info.

**Independent Test**: Log in as admin → create a parent → create a student linked to that parent with skill level, learning speed, status — verify the student appears in the list, can be sorted/filtered by skill level and status, and that logging in as staff hides parent phone/address and tuition.

### Backend — Models, schemas, migrations

- [ ] T038 [P] [US1] Create Parent model in `backend/app/models/parent.py` (id, user_id FK nullable, full_name, phone, phone_secondary, address, zalo_id, notes, timestamps)
- [ ] T039 [P] [US1] Create Student model in `backend/app/models/student.py` (id, parent_id FK, name, nickname, date_of_birth, age, skill_level, personality_notes, learning_speed, current_issues, enrollment_status enum, enrolled_at, timestamps)
- [ ] T040 [P] [US1] Create StudentStatusHistory model in `backend/app/models/student_status_history.py` (id, student_id FK, from_status, to_status, changed_by FK→User, reason, changed_at)
- [ ] T041 [US1] Create migration `backend/alembic/versions/004_parents_students.py` for Parent, Student, StudentStatusHistory with indexes (`parent_id`, `enrollment_status`, `skill_level`, `name` GIN trigram for unaccented search)

### Backend — Schemas + CRUD + endpoints

- [ ] T042 [P] [US1] Create Parent schemas in `backend/app/schemas/parent.py` (ParentCreate, ParentUpdate, ParentRead with role-aware projections — staff response excludes phone/address/zalo_id)
- [ ] T043 [P] [US1] Create Student schemas in `backend/app/schemas/student.py` (StudentCreate, StudentUpdate, StudentRead with embedded parent_name + active_package summary)
- [ ] T044 [US1] Create Parent CRUD in `backend/app/crud/parent.py`
- [ ] T045 [US1] Create Student CRUD in `backend/app/crud/student.py` (filter by status/skill_level/search; sort by name/skill_level/enrolled_at; pagination)
- [ ] T046 [US1] Create student status service in `backend/app/services/student_status_service.py` (writes to StudentStatusHistory on every status change; enforces valid transitions)
- [ ] T047 [US1] Create Parents router in `backend/app/api/parents.py` (`GET /parents`, `GET /parents/{id}`, `POST /parents`, `PATCH /parents/{id}`)
- [ ] T048 [US1] Create Students router in `backend/app/api/students.py` (`GET /students` with filters/sort/pagination, `GET /students/{id}`, `POST /students`, `PATCH /students/{id}`, `PATCH /students/{id}/status`, `GET /students/{id}/status-history`)

### Frontend — Students feature

- [ ] T049 [P] [US1] Create Student API hooks in `frontend/src/features/students/api.js` (useStudentList, useStudent, useCreateStudent, useUpdateStudent, useUpdateStatus)
- [ ] T050 [P] [US1] Create Parent API hooks in `frontend/src/features/students/parentApi.js`
- [ ] T051 [US1] Create StudentTable component in `frontend/src/features/students/StudentTable.jsx` (Ant Design Table, server-side pagination, search input, filter selectors for status/skill_level, role-aware columns)
- [ ] T052 [US1] Create StudentForm component in `frontend/src/features/students/StudentForm.jsx` (covers create + edit; embeds parent picker / parent quick-create modal)
- [ ] T053 [P] [US1] Create ParentFormModal component in `frontend/src/features/students/ParentFormModal.jsx` (used by StudentForm)
- [ ] T054 [US1] Create StudentDetail page in `frontend/src/features/students/StudentDetail.jsx` (tabs: Profile, Status History; conditional sections based on role)
- [ ] T055 [P] [US1] Add student-related translation keys to `frontend/src/i18n/vi.json` and `frontend/src/i18n/en.json` under the `students` namespace

**Checkpoint**: User Story 1 fully functional — admin can manage students end-to-end, staff has restricted view.

---

## Phase 4: User Story 2 — Admin Creates Classes and Schedules (Priority: P1)

**Goal**: Admin can create a class (name, teacher, recurring weekly slot, duration, any number of students). System prevents student double-booking using `[start, start + duration)` overlap. Weekly calendar shows all classes; makeup sessions display a "Makeup" badge.

**Independent Test**: Log in as admin → create a teacher (minimal) → create a class named "Beginner Piano A" with start time 17:00, duration 60 min, 5 students → calendar shows it on the right slot → try to add a 6th student already booked at 17:00 → conflict toast appears.

> The class data model has no `class_type` and no `max_students` per the 2026-04-27 clarification.

### Backend — Teachers (minimal, supporting US2; full CRUD in US5)

- [ ] T056 [P] [US2] Create Teacher model in `backend/app/models/teacher.py` (id, user_id FK nullable, full_name, phone, email, notes, is_active, timestamps)
- [ ] T057 [US2] Create migration `backend/alembic/versions/005_teachers.py`
- [ ] T058 [US2] Create minimal Teacher CRUD in `backend/app/crud/teacher.py` (`list`, `get`, `create`) — full management arrives in US5

### Backend — Classes & enrollment

- [X] T059 [P] [US2] Create ClassSession model in `backend/app/models/class_session.py` with fields: id, teacher_id FK, **name** (required), day_of_week (0–6), start_time (TIME), **duration_minutes** (INTEGER, CHECK > 0), is_recurring (default true), is_makeup (default false), makeup_for_id FK→ClassSession nullable, specific_date nullable, is_active, timestamps. **Do NOT include `class_type` or `max_students`.**
- [ ] T060 [P] [US2] Create ClassEnrollment model in `backend/app/models/class_enrollment.py` (id, class_session_id FK, student_id FK, enrolled_at, is_active) with UNIQUE (class_session_id, student_id)
- [ ] T061 [US2] Create migration `backend/alembic/versions/006_classes_and_enrollment.py` (ClassSession + ClassEnrollment with indexes on teacher_id, day_of_week, (day_of_week, start_time), is_active; UNIQUE enrollment)
- [X] T062 [P] [US2] Create ClassSession schemas in `backend/app/schemas/class_session.py` (ClassSessionCreate with `name`+`duration_minutes`; ClassSessionRead exposing `start_time`, `duration_minutes`, derived `end_time`, embedded teacher + students)
- [X] T063 [US2] Create ClassSession CRUD in `backend/app/crud/class_session.py` (filters: teacher_id, day_of_week, is_active — **no `class_type` filter**)
- [X] T064 [US2] Create scheduling service in `backend/app/services/schedule_service.py`: overlap detection using the half-open range `[start_time, start_time + duration_minutes)` for both student and teacher conflicts; teacher-availability check; **no max-capacity enforcement**
- [X] T065 [US2] Create Classes router in `backend/app/api/classes.py` (`GET /classes`, `GET /classes/{id}`, `POST /classes`, `PATCH /classes/{id}`, `POST /classes/{id}/enroll`, `DELETE /classes/{id}/enroll/{student_id}`) — enroll only returns `409` for time overlap
- [X] T066 [US2] Create Schedule router in `backend/app/api/schedule.py` with `GET /schedule/weekly?week_start=…&teacher_id=…` returning sessions with `name`, `start_time`, `duration_minutes`, derived `end_time`, `is_makeup`, `attendance_marked`

### Migration of any existing data (only run if a prior implementation exists)

- [X] T067 [US2] Create migration `backend/alembic/versions/010_drop_class_type_and_max_students.py` — drops the `class_type` column and enum from `ClassSession`, drops `max_students`, drops `end_time`, adds `name NOT NULL` (backfill from old `title`), adds `duration_minutes NOT NULL CHECK (>0)` (backfill from old `end_time - start_time`). Skip this migration if you are starting from a clean DB at version 006.

### Frontend — Schedule feature

- [ ] T068 [P] [US2] Create Class API hooks in `frontend/src/features/schedule/api.js` (useClassList, useClass, useCreateClass, useUpdateClass, useEnrollStudent, useUnenrollStudent, useWeeklySchedule)
- [X] T069 [US2] Create ClassForm in `frontend/src/features/schedule/ClassForm.jsx` with fields: `name` (text, required), `teacher_id` (select), `day_of_week` (select Mon–Sun), `start_time` (TimePicker, 15-min step), `duration_minutes` (InputNumber, default 60, min 1), `is_recurring` (Switch, default on), `student_ids` (multi-select, **no upper bound**). On `409` conflict, show a toast listing conflicting sessions.
- [X] T070 [US2] Create WeeklyCalendar in `frontend/src/features/schedule/WeeklyCalendar.jsx` using `@fullcalendar/react` with `timeGridWeek` view, range 07:00–21:00. Render each event with the class `name`, teacher name, and student count.
- [X] T071 [US2] Create makeup-badge `eventContent` renderer in `frontend/src/features/schedule/MakeupBadge.jsx` that renders an Ant Design `<Tag color="orange">Makeup</Tag>` (i18n key `schedule.makeupBadge`) above the time range whenever `extendedProps.is_makeup === true` (clarification 2026-04-27)
- [ ] T072 [US2] Create ClassDetail page in `frontend/src/features/schedule/ClassDetail.jsx` (info card, enrolled student list with add/remove, link to attendance for the next occurrence)
- [X] T073 [P] [US2] Add `schedule` namespace keys to `frontend/src/i18n/vi.json` and `en.json` (including `makeupBadge`, conflict messages, form labels)

**Checkpoint**: User Story 2 fully functional — admin can build a complete weekly schedule with conflict detection; makeup sessions are visually distinct.

---

## Phase 5: User Story 3 — Teacher/Admin Takes Attendance (Priority: P1)

**Goal**: Mark per-student attendance per session; absent-with-notice can spawn a makeup; package session count decrements correctly; remaining sessions surface inline.

**Independent Test**: Open today's schedule → mark a class's roster as Present/Absent/Absent-with-Notice → confirm Present students lose 1 session from their active package → schedule a makeup for an absent-with-notice student and verify it appears with a Makeup badge on the next available date.

### Backend — Attendance

- [ ] T074 [P] [US3] Create AttendanceRecord model in `backend/app/models/attendance.py` (id, class_session_id FK, student_id FK, package_id FK nullable, session_date, status enum, makeup_scheduled bool, makeup_session_id FK nullable, marked_by FK→User, notes, timestamps) with UNIQUE (class_session_id, student_id, session_date)
- [ ] T075 [US3] Create migration `backend/alembic/versions/007_attendance.py`
- [ ] T076 [P] [US3] Create Attendance schemas in `backend/app/schemas/attendance.py` (AttendanceRecordCreate, AttendanceBatchCreate, AttendanceRead, MakeupScheduleRequest)
- [ ] T077 [US3] Create attendance service in `backend/app/services/attendance_service.py`: when status=`present`, decrement `Package.remaining_sessions` of the student's active package; when status=`absent_with_notice`, allow makeup creation; package must remain decrementable to negative for owing
- [ ] T078 [US3] Create makeup session service in `backend/app/services/makeup_service.py`: creates a new `ClassSession` with `is_makeup=true`, `makeup_for_id` set, `specific_date` populated; reuses the schedule conflict check from T064
- [ ] T079 [US3] Create Attendance router in `backend/app/api/attendance.py` (`GET /attendance`, `POST /attendance/batch`, `PATCH /attendance/{id}`, `POST /attendance/{id}/makeup`)

### Frontend — Attendance feature

- [ ] T080 [P] [US3] Create Attendance API hooks in `frontend/src/features/attendance/api.js`
- [ ] T081 [US3] Create AttendanceList page in `frontend/src/features/attendance/AttendanceList.jsx` (today's sessions filtered by date; click → batch form)
- [ ] T082 [US3] Create AttendanceBatchForm in `frontend/src/features/attendance/AttendanceBatchForm.jsx` (radio per student: Present / Absent / Absent w/ Notice; shows current `remaining_sessions` per student; "Schedule Makeup" button visible for Absent-with-Notice rows)
- [ ] T083 [US3] Create MakeupSchedulingModal in `frontend/src/features/attendance/MakeupSchedulingModal.jsx` (date + start time + duration picker; submits to `POST /attendance/{id}/makeup`; on success the calendar refreshes and shows the new event with the Makeup badge from T071)
- [ ] T084 [P] [US3] Add `attendance` namespace translation keys to `vi.json` and `en.json`

**Checkpoint**: User Story 3 fully functional — attendance flows drive package decrement and makeup scheduling.

---

## Phase 6: User Story 4 — Admin Manages Tuition Packages (Priority: P1)

**Goal**: Admin assigns packages (12/24/36/custom sessions) with admin-set price (VND), tracks payment status, surfaces renewal reminders, and shows owing students.

**Independent Test**: Assign a 24-session package at 2,400,000 VND to a student, mark as paid → student profile shows package + paid status → bring `remaining_sessions` to 2 (via attendance) → renewal reminder appears in dashboard widget → continue attending past 0 → student is flagged "owing".

### Backend — Packages, payments, reminders

- [ ] T085 [P] [US4] Create Package model in `backend/app/models/package.py` (id, student_id FK, total_sessions CHECK > 0, remaining_sessions, package_type, **price BIGINT NOT NULL CHECK > 0** in VND, payment_status enum, is_active, reminder_status, started_at, expired_at nullable, timestamps)
- [ ] T086 [P] [US4] Create PaymentRecord model in `backend/app/models/payment_record.py` (id, package_id FK, amount BIGINT VND, payment_date, payment_method, notes, recorded_by FK→User, created_at)
- [ ] T087 [P] [US4] Create RenewalReminder model in `backend/app/models/renewal_reminder.py` (id, package_id FK, reminder_number, triggered_at, notification_id FK nullable) with UNIQUE (package_id, reminder_number)
- [ ] T088 [US4] Create migration `backend/alembic/versions/008_packages_payments.py` (Package + PaymentRecord + RenewalReminder with indexes; CHECK constraints)
- [ ] T089 [P] [US4] Create Package + Payment schemas in `backend/app/schemas/package.py` (PackageCreate requires `price`, PackageRead embeds payment summary, PaymentRecordCreate)
- [ ] T090 [US4] Create Package CRUD in `backend/app/crud/package.py` (one active package per student — assigning a new one deactivates the previous; supports filter by payment_status)
- [ ] T091 [US4] Create renewal reminder service in `backend/app/services/renewal_service.py` (idempotent — when `remaining_sessions <= 2` and no `reminded_once` exists, create one; second reminder when remaining_sessions <= 1)
- [ ] T092 [US4] Wire renewal trigger into the attendance service (`backend/app/services/attendance_service.py`) so each `present` write checks the threshold
- [ ] T093 [US4] Create Tuition router in `backend/app/api/tuition.py` (`GET /packages`, `GET /students/{id}/packages`, `POST /packages`, `PATCH /packages/{id}`, `POST /packages/{id}/payments`, `GET /tuition/owing`, `GET /tuition/unpaid`)

### Frontend — Tuition feature

- [ ] T094 [P] [US4] Create Tuition API hooks in `frontend/src/features/tuition/api.js`
- [ ] T095 [US4] Create TuitionList page in `frontend/src/features/tuition/TuitionList.jsx` (filter by paid/unpaid/owing; columns: student, package, remaining sessions, price, status)
- [ ] T096 [US4] Create PackageForm modal in `frontend/src/features/tuition/PackageForm.jsx` (package_type select with 12/24/36/custom; `total_sessions` (auto-filled or editable for custom); `price` numeric in VND with thousand separators; `payment_status`)
- [ ] T097 [US4] Create PackageCard component in `frontend/src/features/tuition/PackageCard.jsx` (progress bar of remaining/total; payment badge; price formatted as `1.200.000 ₫`; red highlight when ≤ 2 remaining; "Owing" tag when negative)
- [ ] T098 [US4] Create PaymentHistory component in `frontend/src/features/tuition/PaymentHistory.jsx` (table of PaymentRecord rows for a package)
- [ ] T099 [P] [US4] Add `tuition` namespace keys to `vi.json` and `en.json`

**Checkpoint**: All four P1 stories are implemented — the system is operationally usable as an MVP.

---

## Phase 7: User Story 5 — Admin Manages Teachers (Priority: P2)

**Goal**: Admin can fully manage teachers and their weekly availability slots. Teacher availability constrains class assignment.

**Independent Test**: Create a teacher with availability Monday 16:00–20:00 → assigning the teacher to a class at 17:00 succeeds → assigning at 21:00 fails with an availability error.

- [ ] T100 [P] [US5] Create TeacherAvailability model in `backend/app/models/teacher_availability.py` (id, teacher_id FK, day_of_week 0–6, start_time, end_time)
- [ ] T101 [US5] Create migration `backend/alembic/versions/009_teacher_availability.py`
- [ ] T102 [P] [US5] Create Teacher + Availability schemas in `backend/app/schemas/teacher.py` (TeacherCreate, TeacherUpdate, TeacherRead with availability list)
- [ ] T103 [US5] Extend Teacher CRUD in `backend/app/crud/teacher.py` (add update/delete; manage availability slots)
- [ ] T104 [US5] Add availability check to schedule service in `backend/app/services/schedule_service.py` (when assigning a teacher to a class, verify the class window is fully inside an availability slot for that day)
- [ ] T105 [US5] Create Teachers router in `backend/app/api/teachers.py` (`GET /teachers`, `GET /teachers/{id}`, `POST /teachers`, `PATCH /teachers/{id}`, `DELETE /teachers/{id}`, `PUT /teachers/{id}/availability`)
- [ ] T106 [P] [US5] Create Teacher API hooks in `frontend/src/features/teachers/api.js`
- [ ] T107 [US5] Create TeacherList page in `frontend/src/features/teachers/TeacherList.jsx`
- [ ] T108 [US5] Create TeacherForm in `frontend/src/features/teachers/TeacherForm.jsx` (basic info)
- [ ] T109 [US5] Create AvailabilityEditor in `frontend/src/features/teachers/AvailabilityEditor.jsx` (per-day time slot list; add/remove)
- [ ] T110 [US5] Create TeacherDetail page in `frontend/src/features/teachers/TeacherDetail.jsx` (info, availability, assigned classes)
- [ ] T111 [P] [US5] Add `teachers` namespace keys to `vi.json` and `en.json`

**Checkpoint**: Teacher management complete; class assignment validates against availability.

---

## Phase 8: User Story 6 — Dashboard Overview (Priority: P2)

**Goal**: At-a-glance operational widgets and quick-action buttons on the home page; revenue widget hidden from staff.

**Independent Test**: Seed data with 25 active students, 3 absences today, 2 students nearing package end → dashboard renders all four widgets with correct counts → log in as staff → revenue widget is absent.

- [ ] T112 [US6] Create dashboard service in `backend/app/services/dashboard_service.py` (active student count, today's session count, today's absence count, students with `remaining_sessions <= 2`, current month revenue from `PaymentRecord`)
- [ ] T113 [US6] Create Dashboard router in `backend/app/api/dashboard.py` (`GET /dashboard/overview` — RBAC-aware; revenue field omitted for staff role)
- [ ] T114 [P] [US6] Create Dashboard API hooks in `frontend/src/features/dashboard/api.js`
- [ ] T115 [US6] Create DashboardPage in `frontend/src/features/dashboard/DashboardPage.jsx` (Ant Design Statistic cards in a Row/Col grid; conditional revenue card)
- [ ] T116 [US6] Create QuickActions component in `frontend/src/features/dashboard/QuickActions.jsx` ("Add Student", "Create Trial Class" buttons that route to the right forms)
- [ ] T117 [P] [US6] Add `dashboard` namespace keys to `vi.json` and `en.json`

**Checkpoint**: All P1 + P2 stories complete; system covers full Phase 1 operations.

---

## Phase 9: User Story 7 — Parent Portal (Priority: P3, Phase 2)

**Goal**: A parent logs into a separate portal and views their children's schedule, attendance history, package info, and (when present) teacher session notes — read-only.

**Independent Test**: Create a Parent user, link to their student, set up some sessions/attendance/notes → log in at `/portal` → verify schedule, attendance history, current package, and notes appear; no edit controls are visible.

- [ ] T118 [US7] Add parent-portal endpoints in `backend/app/api/portal.py` (`GET /portal/me/children`, `GET /portal/children/{id}/schedule`, `GET /portal/children/{id}/attendance`, `GET /portal/children/{id}/package`, `GET /portal/children/{id}/notes`) — all require `role=parent` and enforce that the requested student belongs to the calling parent
- [ ] T119 [US7] Create parent layout in `frontend/src/components/layouts/ParentLayout.jsx` (no admin sidebar; minimal header with language + logout)
- [ ] T120 [P] [US7] Create Portal API hooks in `frontend/src/features/portal/api.js`
- [ ] T121 [US7] Create PortalHome (child list) in `frontend/src/features/portal/PortalHome.jsx`
- [ ] T122 [US7] Create ChildDetail page in `frontend/src/features/portal/ChildDetail.jsx` (tabs: Schedule, Attendance, Package, Notes; all read-only)
- [ ] T123 [P] [US7] Add `portal` namespace keys to `vi.json` and `en.json`

**Checkpoint**: Parent portal MVP delivered.

---

## Phase 10: User Story 8 — Teacher Session Notes (Priority: P3, Phase 2)

**Goal**: After each session, a teacher records lesson content, progress, and homework per student. Notes are visible to admin and (via portal) to parents.

**Independent Test**: As a teacher (or admin acting on their behalf), open a session and record a per-student note → admin sees it on the student profile under "Notes" → parent sees it on the portal under the session date.

- [ ] T124 [P] [US8] Create SessionNote model in `backend/app/models/session_note.py` (id, class_session_id FK, student_id FK, teacher_id FK, session_date, lesson_content, progress_notes, homework, timestamps)
- [ ] T125 [US8] Create migration `backend/alembic/versions/011_session_notes.py`
- [ ] T126 [P] [US8] Create SessionNote schemas in `backend/app/schemas/session_note.py`
- [ ] T127 [US8] Create SessionNotes router in `backend/app/api/session_notes.py` (`POST /session-notes`, `GET /session-notes?student_id=…`, `PATCH /session-notes/{id}`, `GET /students/{id}/notes`)
- [ ] T128 [US8] Wire SessionNote fetch into the parent portal endpoint in `backend/app/api/portal.py` (extend `GET /portal/children/{id}/notes` from T118 to query the SessionNote table)
- [ ] T129 [P] [US8] Create Notes UI component in `frontend/src/features/students/StudentNotesTab.jsx` (timeline of notes by date)
- [ ] T130 [US8] Create SessionNoteForm in `frontend/src/features/attendance/SessionNoteForm.jsx` (per-student note entry attached to a session)

**Checkpoint**: Session notes flow end-to-end (teacher → admin → parent).

---

## Phase 11: User Story 9 — Monthly Reports (Priority: P3, Phase 2)

**Goal**: Auto-generated monthly report covering revenue, new students, attendance rate, and dropout/retention rate — with a revenue bar chart.

**Independent Test**: Backfill a month of payments + attendance + status changes → open `/reports` → all four metrics render → revenue bar chart shows monthly totals over time.

- [ ] T131 [US9] Create reports service in `backend/app/services/reports_service.py` (monthly aggregations: total revenue from PaymentRecord; new students from Student.enrolled_at; attendance rate = present / total marked; dropout = withdrawn this month / active at month start)
- [ ] T132 [US9] Create Reports router in `backend/app/api/reports.py` (`GET /reports/monthly?year=…&month=…`, `GET /reports/revenue-trend?months=12`)
- [ ] T133 [P] [US9] Create Reports API hooks in `frontend/src/features/reports/api.js`
- [ ] T134 [US9] Create ReportsPage in `frontend/src/features/reports/ReportsPage.jsx` (month picker + metric cards)
- [ ] T135 [US9] Create RevenueBarChart component in `frontend/src/features/reports/RevenueBarChart.jsx` (Ant Design Charts or `@ant-design/plots` bar chart of revenue over time)
- [ ] T136 [P] [US9] Add `reports` namespace keys to `vi.json` and `en.json`

**Checkpoint**: Monthly reporting available for admin.

---

## Phase 12: User Story 10 — Automated Notifications (Priority: P3, Phase 2)

**Goal**: Schedule reminders sent to parents via Zalo/SMS before classes; payment due/overdue notifications.

**Independent Test**: Trigger reminder evaluation for tomorrow → a `pending` Notification row is created → after the worker runs, `status=sent` (via mock provider in dev).

- [ ] T137 [P] [US10] Create Notification model in `backend/app/models/notification.py` (id, parent_id FK, type enum, channel enum, status enum, content, reference_id, sent_at, error_message, created_at)
- [ ] T138 [US10] Create migration `backend/alembic/versions/012_notifications.py`
- [ ] T139 [P] [US10] Create Notification schemas in `backend/app/schemas/notification.py`
- [ ] T140 [US10] Create notification provider abstraction in `backend/app/services/notifications/__init__.py` with concrete `ZaloProvider` and `SmsProvider` modules (`zalo_provider.py`, `sms_provider.py`); use a `MockProvider` for dev
- [ ] T141 [US10] Create notification dispatcher service in `backend/app/services/notification_service.py` (queues schedule/payment reminders; consumes `pending` rows)
- [ ] T142 [US10] Create background worker entrypoint in `backend/app/scripts/notification_worker.py` (long-running async loop; dispatches batches; updates row status)
- [ ] T143 [US10] Create Notifications admin router in `backend/app/api/notifications.py` (`GET /notifications`, `POST /notifications/retry/{id}`)
- [ ] T144 [US10] Wire RenewalReminder → Notification by extending `backend/app/services/renewal_service.py` (from T091) so creating a renewal reminder also enqueues a `payment_due` Notification row via the dispatcher from T141

**Checkpoint**: Notification subsystem end-to-end with pluggable providers.

## Phase 13: Polish & Cross-Cutting Concerns

**Purpose**: Hardening, observability, smoke testing, performance, docs

- [ ] T145 [P] Run `quickstart.md` end-to-end on a clean DB and update it where steps drift from reality (especially the class-creation step now uses `name` + `duration_minutes`)
- [ ] T146 [P] Add Playwright smoke spec `frontend/tests/e2e/login-and-schedule.spec.js` covering login → student create → class create with `name`+`duration` → conflict detection → attendance mark → package decrement
- [ ] T147 [P] Add a Playwright spec `frontend/tests/e2e/makeup-badge.spec.js` that schedules a makeup and asserts the orange `Makeup` tag is present on the calendar event (clarification 2026-04-27)
- [ ] T148 [P] Add a backend contract test `backend/tests/contract/test_classes_contract.py` asserting `POST /classes` rejects `class_type` and `max_students` and requires `name`+`duration_minutes`
- [ ] T149 [P] Add an integration test `backend/tests/integration/test_schedule_overlap.py` covering the half-open `[start, start+duration)` overlap rule (boundary cases: back-to-back is fine, 1-minute overlap fails)
- [ ] T150 [P] Add an integration test `backend/tests/integration/test_package_pricing.py` asserting `Package.price > 0` is enforced and PaymentRecord.amount equals package price (clarification 2026-04-27)
- [ ] T151 Review and complete translation coverage: run a script `frontend/scripts/check_i18n.js` that diffs keys between `vi.json` and `en.json` and fails on missing keys (SC-010)
- [ ] T152 Add OpenAPI drift check `backend/tests/contract/test_openapi_snapshot.py` that compares the generated OpenAPI spec against `specs/001-piano-center-management/contracts/api.md` shape
- [ ] T153 [P] Performance: add a database index advisor pass — verify the indexes from `data-model.md` exist; add EXPLAIN-driven indexes for the weekly schedule query
- [ ] T154 [P] Set up structured request logging middleware in `backend/app/main.py` (request id, user id, latency)
- [ ] T155 Document operations in `README.md`: how to run migrations, seed admin, start dev servers, run tests, build PWA for prod
- [ ] T156 Update `.specify/memory/constitution.md` (currently template) — recommended follow-up via `/speckit-constitution` so future specs can be gated against ratified principles

---

## Dependencies & Execution Order

### Phase Dependencies

- **Setup (Phase 1)**: No dependencies — can start immediately
- **Foundational (Phase 2)**: Depends on Setup; **BLOCKS all user stories**
- **User Stories (Phases 3–12)**: All depend on Foundational. Within priority tiers, stories may be done in parallel by different developers
- **Polish (Phase 13)**: Depends on the user stories you intend to ship

### Cross-Story Dependencies

- **US1 (Students)** is required by US2, US3, US4, US7, US8 (all reference Student)
- **US2 (Classes/Schedule)** is required by US3 (attendance attaches to a session) and US8 (notes attach to a session)
- **US4 (Packages)** is required by US3 (attendance decrements packages); US3 may be implemented in parallel using stub package logic and integrated when US4 lands
- **US5 (Teachers)** depends on the minimal Teacher entity created in US2 (T056–T058) but otherwise stands alone
- **US7 (Parent Portal)** depends on US1, US2, US3, US4 — i.e. all P1 stories must exist for the portal to have anything to show
- **US8 (Notes)** is referenced by US7 portal endpoint T128 — implement US8 before final wiring of US7 if both are in scope, or land US7 first with the notes section empty

### Within Each User Story

- Models → migrations → schemas → CRUD → services → routers → frontend hooks → frontend pages
- Tasks marked `[P]` within a story can run in parallel

### Parallel Opportunities

- Setup tasks `[P]` (T003–T009) parallelize freely
- Foundational tasks split cleanly between backend (T011–T025) and frontend (T026–T037)
- Within US1, the three model creations T038/T039/T040 are `[P]`; the schemas T042/T043 are `[P]`; the API hooks T049/T050 are `[P]`
- Within US2, the model creations T056/T059/T060 are `[P]`; the schemas T062 is `[P]` with T060
- Different user stories at the same priority can be worked on by different developers in parallel once the foundational phase is done

---

## Parallel Example: User Story 1

```bash
# After foundational phase, three developers can split US1 work:
# Developer A — models & migration:
Task: "T038 [P] Create Parent model in backend/app/models/parent.py"
Task: "T039 [P] Create Student model in backend/app/models/student.py"
Task: "T040 [P] Create StudentStatusHistory model in backend/app/models/student_status_history.py"
# After all three, Developer A runs T041 (migration).

# Developer B — schemas (parallel with A's models):
Task: "T042 [P] Create Parent schemas in backend/app/schemas/parent.py"
Task: "T043 [P] Create Student schemas in backend/app/schemas/student.py"

# Developer C — frontend wiring (depends on schemas; can start with mocks):
Task: "T049 [P] Create Student API hooks in frontend/src/features/students/api.js"
Task: "T050 [P] Create Parent API hooks in frontend/src/features/students/parentApi.js"
```

---

## Implementation Strategy

### MVP First (User Story 1 only)

1. Complete Phase 1: Setup
2. Complete Phase 2: Foundational
3. Complete Phase 3: User Story 1 — Admin Manages Students
4. **STOP and VALIDATE**: log in, create a student, sort/filter, switch to staff and verify hidden fields
5. Demo / deploy

### Incremental P1 Delivery (true Phase 1 MVP)

1. Setup + Foundational
2. US1 → demo
3. US2 → demo (now you have classes + schedule)
4. US3 → demo (attendance flows)
5. US4 → demo (full operational MVP — Phase 1 complete)

### Phase 2 (deferred / parallel)

- US5 (Teachers full mgmt) can land any time after US2 (depends only on the minimal Teacher from US2)
- US6 (Dashboard) needs real data from US1–US4 to be meaningful
- US7–US10 are explicitly Phase 2 features per the spec — schedule them after the Phase 1 MVP is in production

### Parallel Team Strategy

With 3+ developers after Foundational:

- Dev A → US1 (Students)
- Dev B → US2 (Classes/Schedule) — minimal Teacher model unblocks Dev C
- Dev C → US5 once T056–T058 land; otherwise pair on US2

---

## Notes

- `[P]` = different files, no incomplete dependencies
- `[Story]` label maps each task to a user story for traceability
- Each user story can be implemented and validated independently after the Foundational phase
- Commit per task or per logical group — auto-commit hook is configured in `.specify/extensions.yml`
- Stop at any checkpoint to validate the story in isolation
- Avoid: vague tasks, two tasks editing the same file in parallel, or cross-story dependencies that break independence
- Tests beyond the Polish-phase smoke set are intentionally omitted — re-run `/speckit-tasks` with an explicit TDD request if a test-first workflow is desired
